subject: Mathematics
title: Alan Turing: Code Breaker and Computer Pioneer
description: Learn Alan Turing: Code Breaker and Computer Pioneer in one minute.

hook.text: Alan Turing broke the Nazi Enigma code—and in doing so, shortened World War II by years.

buildup.text: Before the war, he defined the theoretical computer. His Turing machine formalized computation itself.

discovery.text: He proved that some problems are impossible for any computer to solve—a profound limit on machines.

twist.text: The British government prosecuted him for being gay. He was chemically castrated and died at 41.

climax.text: In 2013, Queen Elizabeth granted him a royal pardon. His face now appears on the British 50-pound note.

punchline.text: He saved millions of lives and was punished for who he loved.

quiz.question: What did Turing's theoretical machine formalize?
